payload.query: In recent decades, we have seen a rise in the adoption of gender quotas. Scholars often debate the factors that explain quota adoption. Based on your understanding of the literature, what is the most salient factor(s) that explain quota adoption?

articles.title: Read the Supreme Court Decision
articles.abstract: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.
articles.snippet: The Supreme Court ruled that the race-conscious admissions programs at Harvard and the University of North Carolina were unlawful, curtailing affirmative action at colleges and universities around the nation.

articles.title: Ruth Bader Ginsburg, Supreme Court’s Feminist Icon, Is Dead at 87
articles.abstract: The second woman appointed to the Supreme Court, Justice Ginsburg’s pointed and powerful dissenting opinions earned her late-life rock stardom.
articles.snippet: The second woman appointed to the Supreme Court, Justice Ginsburg’s pointed and powerful dissenting opinions earned her late-life rock stardom.

articles.title: Are You ‘Biohacking’ Your Diet? We Want to Hear From You.
articles.abstract: Your responses may be used in an article that examines the growing popularity of biohacking.

articles.title: Two Stunning Picture Books That Treat Tantrums With Tenderness
articles.abstract: Julie Fogliano and Marla Frazee’s “Because of a Shoe” and Beatrice Alemagna’s “Her Muddy Majesty of Muck” address children’s anger with compassion.
articles.snippet: Julie Fogliano and Marla Frazee’s “Because of a Shoe” and Beatrice Alemagna’s “Her Muddy Majesty of Muck” address children’s anger with compassion.

articles.title: When Life Gave Her Ageism, She Created Oldster
articles.abstract: Sari Botton started a Substack about getting older after finding employers were reluctant to hire her, a middle-aged woman. With more than 70,000 subscribers, she has clearly struck a nerve.
articles.snippet: Sari Botton started a Substack about getting older after finding employers were reluctant to hire her, a middle-aged woman. With more than 70,000 subscribers, she has clearly struck a nerve.

articles.title: From 2019: Toni Morrison, Towering Novelist of the Black Experience, Dies at 88
articles.abstract: Ms. Morrison, who wrote “Beloved” and “Song of Solomon,” was the first African-American woman to win the Nobel in literature.
articles.snippet: Ms. Morrison, who wrote “Beloved” and “Song of Solomon,” was the first African-American woman to win the Nobel in literature.

articles.title: Trump Has Lost Touch With Reality
articles.abstract: The president is becoming the very thing that destroyed the Democrats: a reality-denying machine.
articles.snippet: The president is becoming the very thing that destroyed the Democrats: a reality-denying machine.
articles.byline: By Frank Bruni and Bret Stephens

articles.title: Daryl Hannah: How Can ‘Love Story’ Get Away With This?
articles.abstract: The character “Daryl Hannah” in “Love Story” is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life or my conduct — and these kinds of lies don’t go away.
articles.snippet: The character “Daryl Hannah” in “Love Story” is not even a remotely accurate representation of my life or my conduct — and these kinds of lies don’t go away.
